speaker: Guest
text_en: undeclared wars are commonplace. Tragically, our government engages in preemptive war otherwise known as aggression with no complaints from the American people.
text: 未宣战的战争早已司空见惯。可悲的是，我们的政府发动先发制人的战争，也就是所谓的侵略，而美国民众对此却毫无怨言。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Sadly, we have become accustomed to living with the illegitimate use of force by government. To develop a truly free society, the issue of initiating force must be understood and rejected.
text: 令人遗憾的是，我们已经习惯了政府以不正当方式使用武力。要建立一个真正自由的社会，就必须理解并拒绝主动使用武力这个问题。

speaker: Guest
text_en: What if sometimes to love your country you had to alter or abolish the government? What if Jefferson was right? What if that government is best which governs least?
text: 如果有时候，爱你的国家就意味着你必须改革甚至废除政府呢？如果 Jefferson 是对的呢？如果最好的政府就是管得最少的政府呢？

speaker: Guest
text_en: What if it is dangerous to be right when the government is wrong? What if it is better to perish fighting for freedom than to live as a slave?
text: 如果当政府错了的时候，做对的事反而会有危险呢？如果为自由而战、哪怕牺牲，也比像奴隶一样活着更好呢？

speaker: Guest
text_en: What if freedom's greatest hour of danger is now?
text: 如果自由最危险的时刻就是现在呢？

speaker: Host
text_en: Hi, everyone. Judge Andrew Napolitano here for Judging Freedom. Today is Tuesday, April 28th, 2026.
text: 大家好，我是 Andrew Napolitano 法官，这里是 Judging Freedom。今天是 2026 年 4 月 28 日，星期二。

speaker: Host
text_en: Professor John Mearsheimer will be with us in just a moment. Just how did Donald Trump lose his war of choosing?
text: John Mearsheimer 教授马上就到。Donald Trump 究竟是怎么输掉他主动选择发动的那场战争的？

speaker: Host
text_en: But first this.
text: 不过先看这个。

speaker: Host
text_en: Don't you just cringe when people say, I told you so. Sorry, I told you gold and silver would reap the benefits due to excessive money printing, inflation and global uncertainty. It's here. It's happened. Gold and silver reached all time highs.
text: 当别人说“我早就告诉过你了”，你是不是也会尴尬得直起鸡皮疙瘩？抱歉，我早就说过，黄金和白银会因为过度印钞、通胀和全球不确定性而受益。现在来了，已经发生了。黄金和白银都创下了历史新高。

speaker: Host
text_en: Did you call Lear Capital and buy some? It's not too late. Experts are predicting higher prices ahead. Why? Nothing has changed. Geopolitical chaos, cost of living crises, and a weaker dollar are driving central banks to boost their gold reserves.
text: 你给 Lear Capital 打电话买一些了吗？现在还不算晚。专家预测，接下来价格还会更高。为什么？因为什么都没变。地缘政治动荡、生活成本危机以及美元走弱，正推动各国央行增加黄金储备。

speaker: Host
text_en: Forecasts suggest gold could hit $6,000 an ounce and silver $200 an ounce. Even Morgan Stanley ditched the 60-40 rule for 60-20-20, putting 20% into precious metals. They're getting educated, and you should too. Call the best in the business.
text: 预测显示，黄金可能涨到每盎司 6000 美元，白银可能涨到每盎司 200 美元。就连 Morgan Stanley 都放弃了 60 比 40 的配置规则，改成了 60-20-20，把 20% 配到贵金属上。他们已经开始补课了，你也该这样。给业内最靠谱的人打电话。

speaker: Host
text_en: As in the people I trust, Lear Capital. Get their reports, get the facts, get some gold and silver. Tell them the judge sent you and get up to $20,000 in bonus gold or silver. Call 800-511-4620 or go to leargjudgenap.com.
text: 也就是我信任的那家公司，Lear Capital。拿他们的报告，了解事实，买一些黄金和白银。告诉他们是 the judge 介绍你来的，你最高可以拿到价值 2 万美元的额外黄金或白银。拨打 800-511-4620，或者访问 leargjudgenap.com。

speaker: Host
text_en: Professor Mearsheimer, welcome here, my dear friend. As always, thank you for accommodating the schedule. How significant?
text: Mearsheimer 教授，欢迎您，我亲爱的朋友。和往常一样，感谢您配合这个时间安排。这件事有多重要？

speaker: Host
text_en: In your view, is the trip by Iranian Foreign Minister Arachi to Moscow where he was personally and rather ostentatiously greeted by President Putin himself?
text: 在您看来，伊朗外长 Arachi 此次访问莫斯科，而且是由 Putin 总统亲自、并且相当高调地出面迎接，这意味着什么？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, I think it has implications beyond the Iran war, and it has implications for U.S.-Russian relations and for what's going on with regard to Ukraine. I think if you listen carefully to what the Russians are saying these days,
text: 我认为，这件事的影响不只是伊朗战争本身，它还关系到美俄关系，也关系到乌克兰局势的发展。我觉得，如果你仔细听听俄罗斯人最近在说什么，

speaker: Guest
text_en: and especially a speech that Lavrov gave on April 24th, you see that the Russians have basically given up on the Americans. They understand that they're at war with the West.
text: 尤其是 Lavrov 在 4 月 24 日发表的那场讲话，你就会发现，俄罗斯人基本上已经对美国人不抱希望了。他们明白，自己是在和整个西方交战。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Nothing has changed despite all President Trump's promises of settling the Ukraine war and improving relations between the United States and Russia. That really hasn't happened. And we still have our gun sights on the Russians.
text: 尽管 President Trump 一再承诺要解决乌克兰战争、改善美国和俄罗斯的关系，但其实什么都没变。这些根本没有发生。我们依然把枪口对准俄罗斯人。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And that's driven the Russians and the Iranians close together. And I think what you see when Arachi goes to St.
text: 而这也让俄罗斯和伊朗越走越近。我觉得你看到 Arachi 去 St.

speaker: Guest
text_en: Petersburg to talk to Putin and they have this long discussion is evidence of that tight relationship that has developed between the two sides. And I would note very importantly that there's all sorts of evidence that the Russians are helping Iranians.
text: Petersburg 和 Putin 会谈，而且两人进行了长时间讨论，这就说明双方已经形成了非常紧密的关系。我还想特别指出，有各种证据表明俄罗斯人在帮助伊朗人。

speaker: Guest
text_en: With their use of drones and ballistic missiles against U.S., Israeli, and Gulf State forces,
text: 在他们针对美国、以色列以及海湾国家部队使用无人机和弹道导弹这件事上，

speaker: Guest
text_en: it's quite clear that the Russians are providing satellite data and they're also providing computer chips for the drones and the missiles that work with that satellite data to make Iran's drones and missiles deadly accurate. So we are up against a very formidable.
text: 很明显，俄罗斯在提供卫星数据，也在提供用于无人机和导弹的计算机芯片，这些武器会结合卫星数据使用，从而让伊朗的无人机和导弹具备致命的精准度。所以我们面对的是一支非常难对付的。

speaker: Guest
text_en: double fighting force in the Gulf in good part because the Russians are supporting the Iranians and again the Iranians and the Russians have been driven together there's hardly any daylight between them these
text: 海湾地区的双重作战力量，很大程度上就是因为俄罗斯在支持伊朗。而且再次强调，伊朗和俄罗斯已经被推到了一起，如今两者之间几乎没有任何距离。这些

speaker: Host
text_en: Right.
text: 对。

speaker: Guest
text_en: days there's our policies vis-a-vis not only the Russians but the Iranians as well
text: 天以来，我们针对的不只是俄罗斯，也包括伊朗，我们的政策就是如此。

speaker: Host
text_en: So these photos that we're watching or the videos that we're watching, there's...
text: 所以我们现在看到的这些照片，或者说我们在看的这些视频里，有……

speaker: Host
text_en: Foreign Minister's Rodger and Lavrov and recognized President Putin. Is this performative or substantive that Putin himself would get involved?
text: 有外长 Rodger 和 Lavrov，还有公认的总统 Putin。Putin 本人亲自介入，这到底是在作秀，还是有实质意义？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Though it's definitely substantive. I mean, this is high stakes diplomacy. I mean, this really involves issues that matter enormously.
text: 我觉得这肯定是有实质意义的。我的意思是，这是高风险的外交博弈。这里面牵涉的，确实都是极其重要的问题。

speaker: Guest
text_en: What I find very interesting in addition to the substance is just how optimistic or cheerful the Russians and the Iranians appear. I think in both cases, they understand. And they have the Americans on their back heels,
text: 除了实质内容之外，我觉得很有意思的一点是，俄罗斯人和伊朗人看起来是多么乐观、甚至有些轻松。我觉得双方都明白，而且他们让美国人处在了被动防守的位置，

speaker: Guest
text_en: that we're in deep trouble in the Middle East and we're in deep trouble in Ukraine and that they are in many ways in the driver's seat. So you don't see any evidence of deep concern,
text: 也就是我们在中东陷入了很大的麻烦，在乌克兰也陷入了很大的麻烦，而他们在很多方面都掌握着主动权。所以你看不出他们有任何深切担忧的迹象，

speaker: Guest
text_en: of pessimism on the part of either one of those two leaders in the video that you're showing.
text: 从你放的这段视频里，也看不出这两位领导人任何一方有悲观情绪。

speaker: Host
text_en: Right. Right.
text: 对，对。

speaker: Guest
text_en: The Iranians have.
text: 伊朗人已经……

speaker: Host
text_en: reiterated to the americans apparently in whatever the most recent communication was whether it was direct or through the pakistanis i don't know their steadfast and consistent position that they will not voluntarily give up on the right to enrich uranium for civilian purposes does the u.s take this seriously do they recognize that other countries have red lines you
text: 显然，在最近一次沟通中——不管是直接沟通，还是通过巴基斯坦人传话，我不清楚——他们再次向美国人重申了自己坚定而一贯的立场：他们不会自愿放弃为民用目的进行铀浓缩的权利。美国认真对待这一点吗？他们是否承认别的国家也有红线，有自己的想法，

speaker: Host
text_en: Minds just like we do.
text: 就像我们一样。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, the United States is not very good at accepting the idea that other countries have red lines. We think it's our way or the highway.
text: 嗯，美国并不擅长接受别的国家也有红线这个观念。我们总觉得要么按我们的方式来，要么就别谈。

speaker: Guest
text_en: That's certainly been the case on the nuclear enrichment issue. We were forced to compromise somewhat on that issue with the JCPOA. The Israelis didn't like that, and they, I think, convinced Trump that the JCPOA was therefore a bad agreement.
text: 在核浓缩问题上尤其如此。在 JCPOA 这件事上，我们当时被迫在这个问题上做出了一些妥协。以色列人不喜欢那样，我认为他们也说服了 Trump，让他觉得 JCPOA 因此是个糟糕的协议。

speaker: Guest
text_en: agreement that he of course pulled out of it in 2018 but I think what's most interesting here is that What the Americans want to do is they want to start by negotiating on the nuclear issue, the nuclear enrichment issue, as you put it.
text: 而他当然也在 2018 年退出了这项协议。不过我觉得这里最有意思的是，美国人想做的是先从核问题谈起，也就是像你说的，先谈铀浓缩问题。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But with the latest proposal that's come out of Iran, they've moved the nuclear enrichment table to number three on the list of issues that have to be dealt with. They say that first of all, what we have to do is we have to have a meaningful peace.
text: 但随着伊朗最新提出的方案，他们已经把核浓缩这个议题挪到了必须处理的问题清单中的第三位。他们说，首先，我们必须实现有意义的和平。

speaker: Guest
text_en: We have to shut down this conflict, and there has to be some guarantee that the United States and Israel are not going to attack Iran again. That's the first demand. The second demand is that they'll then talk about the Strait of Hormuz.
text: 我们必须结束这场冲突，而且还必须有某种保证，确保美国和以色列不会再次攻击伊朗。这是第一个要求。第二个要求是，之后他们才会谈 Hormuz 海峡。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And of course, it's very clear that they are going to maintain control of the Strait of Hormuz for as far as the eye can see. So they're not going to make any concessions to us on that one. And then number three, they'll be able,
text: 当然，现在已经非常清楚，他们会在可预见的未来继续控制 Hormuz 海峡。所以在这一点上，他们不会对我们做出任何让步。然后第三，他们才会能够，

speaker: Guest
text_en: they'll be willing to talk about the nuclear issue. So in other words, you have to basically shut down the conflict, then deal with the issue of the strait, and then we can talk about the nuclear issue. This is not what the Americans want.
text: 他们才会愿意谈核问题。换句话说，你基本上得先让冲突停下来，然后处理海峡问题，之后我们才能谈核问题。这不是美国人想要的。

speaker: Guest
text_en: The Americans want that nuclear issue front and center. But all of this shows you that as time goes by, the Iranians feel more and more more confident about their position. This gets back to my earlier point about the smiling faces in
text: 美国人想把核问题放在最核心、最优先的位置。但这一切都说明，随着时间推移，伊朗人对自己的处境越来越有信心。这又回到我之前提到的 St. Petersburg 里那些带着笑容的面孔。

speaker: Host
text_en: Right
text: 对。

speaker: Guest
text_en: St. Petersburg. And as I've said many times on this show, time is on Iran's side. There's no rush, in my opinion, for Iran to cut a deal. Just let time go by and let the economic consequences of this war take effect.
text: 而且我在这个节目里已经说过很多次了，时间站在伊朗这一边。在我看来，伊朗根本不着急达成协议。就让时间过去，让这场战争带来的经济后果慢慢显现出来。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And that will put greater and greater pressure on the Americans. Americans settle, and that'll give the Iranians more leverage. So what you see happening here is with this new three-point plan, the Iranians are actually driving a harder bargain.
text: 那样会给美国人带来越来越大的压力。美国人最后会妥协，而这会让伊朗人拥有更大的筹码。所以你现在看到的是，借着这个新的三点方案，伊朗人其实是在更强硬地谈条件。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And of course, President Trump is deeply dissatisfied with this new three-point proposal that the Iranians have put forward because, as I said, he wants to get the nuclear issue settled first.
text: 当然，Trump 总统对伊朗方面提出的这个新三点提案非常不满，因为正如我刚才说的，他想先把核问题解决掉。

speaker: Host
text_en: Here's Foreign Minister Arachi yesterday thanking the Russians, cut number 12.
text: 这是外交部长 Arachi 昨天感谢俄罗斯的讲话，来看第 12 段。

speaker: Host
text_en: As you noted, the entire world, it was proven to the entire world that the Iranian people, with their courage, with their resistance against the American onslaught, against the American aggression, will...
text: 正如你指出的，整个世界都已经看到，也向整个世界证明了，伊朗人民凭借他们的勇气，凭借他们在面对美国猛攻、面对美国侵略时的抵抗，将会……

speaker: Host
text_en: withstand these trials this period of time.
text: 挺过这些考验，熬过这段时期。

speaker: Host
text_en: And also it was proven to everybody that Tehran has friends and allies such as Russia that in the times of need are standing next to Iran and we are grateful to you for your support and of the Islamic Republic of Iran.
text: 而且这也向所有人证明了，Tehran 是有朋友和盟友的，比如 Russia，在关键时刻会站在 Iran 身边。我们感谢你们对伊朗伊斯兰共和国的支持。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, a decent statement and one that you would expect he would make publicly towards the end of their meeting.
text: 嗯，这番表态还算得体，而且也是你会预料到他会在会谈接近尾声时公开说的话。

speaker: Guest
text_en: On Sunday, Foreign Minister or Foreign Minister, forgive me, Secretary of State Rubio made some comments that I thought were irrational. It was odd for him to be talking about.
text: 周日，Foreign Minister，不对，应该说 Secretary of State Rubio 发表了一些我认为不太理性的评论。他谈起这个让我觉得很奇怪。

speaker: Guest
text_en: negotiations with Iran is totally out of the picture. He hasn't been in Islamabad.
text: 说什么和 Iran 的谈判完全不在考虑范围内。他根本就没去 Islamabad。

speaker: Guest
text_en: As far as we know, he doesn't participate in any of the negotiations, unlike all of his predecessors who'd be front and center because the president has chosen his two allies and his son-in-law and his former business partner.
text: 据我们所知，他并没有参与任何谈判；不像他之前的历任官员那样都会站在最前面，因为总统选择了他的两个盟友、他的女婿，还有他以前的生意伙伴。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But tell me what you think of this. Vice President Rubio about the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, cut number 10.
text: 不过你跟我说说你怎么看这个。关于 Hormuz Strait，Vice President Rubio 周日的那番话，来放第10段。

speaker: Host
text_en: On Saturday, the President canceled the talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, keeping special envoys Kushner and Whitkoff here in the United States. He said just ten minutes later the Iranians made a new offer on paper, and it was better.
text: 周六，总统取消了在 Pakistan 的 Islamabad 举行的会谈，把特使 Kushner 和 Whitkoff 留在了美国国内。他说，仅仅十分钟后，Iran 方面就提交了一份新的书面提议，而且条件更好了。

speaker: Host
text_en: If what they mean by opening the straits is, yes, the straits are open as long as you coordinate with Iran, get our permission or we'll blow you up and you pay us, that's not opening the straits. Those are international waterways.
text: 如果他们所谓的开放海峡，意思是：没错，海峡是开放的，但前提是你得和 Iran 协调，得到我们的许可，不然我们就把你炸掉，而且你还得给我们交钱——那根本不叫开放海峡。那是国际水道。

speaker: Host
text_en: They cannot normalize nor can we tolerate them trying to normalize a system in which the Iranians decide who gets to use an international waterway and how much you have to pay them to use it.
text: 他们不能把这种做法变成常态，我们也不能容忍他们试图把这样一种体系常态化——由伊朗来决定谁能使用国际水道，以及使用它要付他们多少钱。

speaker: Host
text_en: I think I inadvertently called him Vice President Rubio. He, of course, is the Secretary of State. What is he forgetting, that the United States is trying to block the Strait of Hormuz as well?
text: 我刚才好像一不小心把他叫成了副总统 Rubio。其实他当然是国务卿。他是不是忘了，美国其实也在试图封锁 Hormuz 海峡？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Of course. But I just want to point out that he talked about this new offer that's supposed to be better than previous offers. As I described it a few minutes ago, the new offer is not better.
text: 当然。不过我只是想指出，他提到了这个所谓比之前方案更好的新提议。就像我几分钟前说的，这个新提议并没有更好。

speaker: Guest
text_en: It's a worse offer. And what he doesn't understand and the president doesn't understand is that the Iranians are not going to give up control of the Strait. They'd be crazy to give up control of the Strait. Not only in the midst of this conflict,
text: 这是个更差的提议。而且他不明白、总统也不明白的是，伊朗人不可能放弃对海峡的控制。他们要是放弃对海峡的控制，那才真是疯了。不只是眼下这场冲突期间，

speaker: Guest
text_en: but for as far as the eye can see. It is where their leverage comes from. The control of the Strait plus all those missiles, both cruise missiles, ballistic missiles, and of course drones that they have.
text: 而且在可预见的未来都不会。这就是他们筹码的来源。对海峡的控制，再加上他们手里的那些导弹——包括巡航导弹、弹道导弹，当然还有无人机。

speaker: Guest
text_en: They're not going to give those things up. That's their leverage. And by the way, with regard to Secretary of State Rubio and his absence from the negotiating process.
text: 他们不会放弃这些东西的。这就是他们的筹码。顺便说一句，关于国务卿 Rubio 没有参与谈判这件事。

speaker: Guest
text_en: You want to understand that there are two members of the administration who desperately want to become president in 2028. One is Marco Rubio and the other is J.D. Vance. And both of them understand that if you get involved in these negotiations,
text: 你得明白，政府里有两个人极度想在 2028 年当上总统。一个是 Marco Rubio，另一个是 J.D. Vance。而且他们俩都明白，如果你卷入这些谈判，

speaker: Guest
text_en: you in the end are going to end up making significant concessions to the Iranians because the Iranians are in the driver's seat here. We've basically lost this war.
text: 到头来，你还是会不得不对伊朗人做出重大让步，因为现在掌握主动权的是伊朗人。这场战争我们基本上已经输了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And if you make those concessions, if you're the point person for making those concessions, you're going to take a lot of heat from the Israel lobby. You're going to be in trouble with the lobby and that's going to make it hard to become president.
text: 而如果你做出这些让步，如果你是那个负责拍板这些让步的人，你就会承受来自 Israel lobby 的巨大压力。你会跟这个 lobby 闹僵，这也会让你更难当上总统。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So you can rest assured that J.D. Vance has no interest in going to Islamabad and being the point man in the negotiations with the Iranians.
text: 所以你完全可以放心，J.D. Vance 对去 Islamabad、充当与伊朗人谈判的主谈人毫无兴趣。

speaker: Host
text_en: I don't know if he can wash that. That stain off his hands. I agree with you, but he might be indelibly damaged already.
text: 我不知道他还能不能把这件事洗干净，把手上的这个污点抹掉。我同意你的看法，但他可能已经留下无法磨灭的伤害了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I think that may be true for sure. But I would just say to you,
text: 我觉得这很可能确实是这样。不过我还是想跟你说，

speaker: Guest
text_en: Rubio has done a brilliant job given the fact that he's Secretary of State and National Security Advisor at staying out of these negotiations because he fully understands, I am sure, that he just doesn't want to get involved because in the end we're going to lose the lobby and Israel itself are going to be outraged by any compromises.
text: Rubio 干得非常漂亮。考虑到他既是 Secretary of State 又是 National Security Advisor，他一直设法不卷入这些谈判，因为我敢肯定他完全明白，自己就是不想牵扯进去，因为到最后我们会输，而 lobby 和 Israel 本身都会对任何妥协感到愤怒。

speaker: Guest
text_en: that we make and whoever actually makes those compromises is going to pay a significant political price.
text: 我们做出的任何妥协，真正去做这些妥协的人，都会付出相当大的政治代价。

speaker: Host
text_en: From the Iranian perspective, surely they know that they're negotiating with Netanyahu's puppets.
text: 从伊朗人的角度看，他们肯定也知道，自己是在跟 Netanyahu 的傀儡谈判。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Oh, absolutely. They've made that clear. That's why they want Vance to come to the negotiations. They know that Kushner is basically an Israeli cutout and that not only is Wittkopf a Zionist, but he's demonstrated quite
text: 哦，当然。他们已经把这点说得很清楚了。所以他们才想让 Vance 来参加谈判。他们知道 Kushner 基本上就是以色列的代理人，而且 Wittkopf 不光是个 Zionist，还已经相当明显地表现出——

speaker: Guest
text_en: clearly both in his negotiations with the Iranians and the Russians that he's massively incompetent. So they don't want to deal with those two. They want to deal with Vance.
text: 不管是在跟伊朗人还是俄罗斯人谈判时，他都极其无能。所以他们不想跟那两个人打交道，他们想跟 Vance 谈。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And they did deal with Vance over the weekend of April 11th, 12th. That did not work out very well. I mean,
text: 而且他们确实在 4 月 11 日、12 日那个周末跟 Vance 接触过。结果并不怎么好。我的意思是，

speaker: Guest
text_en: what has to happen here is that the United States has to feel more pain and the world has to feel more pain and put pressure on the United States. to end this as quickly as possible before we're going to get any kind of meaningful negotiations.
text: 现在必须发生的是，美国得感受到更多痛苦，世界也得感受到更多痛苦，并向美国施压，尽快结束这件事。否则，我们根本不会看到任何有意义的谈判。

speaker: Host
text_en: How did Trump lose his war of choosing? Poor preparation, no articulation of achievable military goals, going too quickly, his hand forced by Netanyahu. What do you think?
text: Trump 是怎么输掉这场他自己选择发动的战争的？准备不足、没有明确可实现的军事目标、推进得太快、又被 Netanyahu 逼着出手。你怎么看？

speaker: Guest
text_en: He had a strategy that was guaranteed to fail.
text: 他的策略从一开始就注定会失败。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I mean, the Israelis convinced President Trump that if we and they attacked Iran with a shock and awe campaign and sitting at the center of that shock and awe campaign was a decapitation strategy, that that would cause the regime to collapse.
text: 我的意思是，以色列人说服了 Trump 总统，让他相信如果我们和他们一起对 Iran 发动一场“震慑打击”行动，而这场行动的核心是一套“斩首”战略，那么这就会导致该政权垮台。

speaker: Guest
text_en: and put in place a new regime that would dance to our tune. And this would be very easy to do. We would win a quick and decisive victory. And you want to remember.
text: 然后扶植一个会听我们摆布的新政权。而且这会非常容易做到。我们会迅速而决定性地获胜。你要记住。

speaker: Guest
text_en: That virtually all of President Trump's advisors told him that this was ridiculous. The only possible exception is Pete Hexeth. But as I've said before,
text: 实际上，Trump 总统的几乎所有顾问都告诉他，这事很荒唐。唯一可能的例外是 Pete Hexeth。但正如我之前说过的，

speaker: Guest
text_en: Pete Hexeth is not a serious strategist. And I don't believe even Trump takes him that seriously. But General Kang, John Ratcliffe, the head of the CIA, Vance himself, and Rubio all said that the strategy that the Israelis had.
text: Pete Hexeth 不是个严肃的战略家。我甚至不觉得连 Trump 自己都那么当回事。但 General Kang、CIA 负责人 John Ratcliffe、Vance 本人，还有 Rubio，都说以色列人提出的那套战略

speaker: Guest
text_en: as it put forward, was not likely to work. It's a very interesting case because the deep state and the president's principal American advisors warned him against executing the strategy that the Israelis had come up with.
text: 不太可能奏效。这是个很有意思的案例，因为所谓的深层政府，以及总统身边最主要的美国顾问，都警告他不要执行以色列人想出来的这套战略。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But Trump decided for one reason or another that he was going to go along with the Israelis and they executed a strategy and I want to be clear here that was guaranteed to fail.
text: 但 Trump 出于某种原因，还是决定跟着以色列人走，他们也执行了那套战略。我想在这里说清楚，那套战略注定会失败。

speaker: Guest
text_en: We have no instance in recorded history of an air campaign alone toppling a regime and that leading to a favorable outcome. It just doesn't work. You can't use air power alone to create regime change and win a war in a couple days or a week's time.
text: 在有记录的历史上，我们找不出任何一个只靠空袭就推翻一个政权、而且还带来有利结果的案例。这根本行不通。你不可能只靠空中力量就在几天或者一周内实现政权更迭并打赢一场战争。

speaker: Guest
text_en: It just doesn't happen. And I think all of his advisors. understood that but the president allowed himself to be bamboozled by BB Netanyahu and the head of Mossad as well David Barnea who was a key player and
text: 这种事就是不会发生。我认为他的所有顾问都明白这一点，但总统还是让自己被 BB Netanyahu 以及 Mossad 负责人 David Barnea 给忽悠了，David Barnea 也是其中的关键人物，而且

speaker: Guest
text_en: I mean,
text: 我的意思是，

speaker: Guest
text_en: he is paying the price for that I should say we are paying the price
text: 他正在为此付出代价——应该说，是我们在为此付出代价。

speaker: Host
text_en: yes,
text: 对。

speaker: Guest
text_en: for that
text: 在这件事上。

speaker: Host
text_en: we are. Do you think that his advisors, because of his volcanic personality, extraordinary view of himself.
text: 我们确实是这么看。你觉得他的顾问们，会不会因为他那种火山般的性格、对自己极其夸大的看法，

speaker: Host
text_en: intellectual vapidity, refusal to read all these things taken together. Do you think his advisors are afraid to contradict him to his face or afraid to contradict Netanyahu?
text: 思想上的空洞、拒绝阅读，把这些因素都加在一起，你觉得他的顾问们是不敢当面反驳他，还是不敢反驳 Netanyahu？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Sure, there's just no question about that. And again, you want to remember that two of the principal advisors of the four that I mentioned are Marco Rubio and J.D. Vance, and they both want to be president of the United States. And therefore,
text: 当然，这一点毫无疑问。而且还是那句话，你得记住，我提到的那四个主要顾问里，有两个是 Marco Rubio 和 J.D. Vance，他们俩都想当美国总统。所以，

speaker: Guest
text_en: they want to remain in the good graces of Donald Trump and in the good graces of Israel. They are in many ways in a straitjacket. There are just limits to what they can do. And when President Trump decides he wants to do something,
text: 他们都想继续得到 Donald Trump 的欢心，也想继续得到 Israel 的欢心。在很多方面，他们其实是被束缚住了。他们能做的事情就是有限的。而当 Trump 总统决定要做某件事时，

speaker: Guest
text_en: something and Netanyahu is in favor of that same strategy. There are just real limits to what somebody like Rubio or Vance can do to fight with the president to tell him that this is a really bad idea. And I think, by the way, General Kane,
text: 而且 Netanyahu 也支持同样的策略时，那像 Rubio 或 Vance 这样的人，想跟总统据理力争、告诉他这是个非常糟糕的主意，实际上能做的也非常有限。顺便说一句，我觉得 General Kane，

speaker: Guest
text_en: who many people badmouth, has, in my opinion, behaved admirably here, as best I can tell from what's in the public record. Cane, who was Trump's
text: 很多人都在说他的坏话，但依我看，就公开记录所显示的情况而言，他在这件事上的表现是值得称赞的。Kane，他是 Trump 的

speaker: Guest
text_en: handpicked chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff has told Trump at numerous points along the way that we did not have the capability to achieve the goals that he was pursuing, he meaning Donald Trump. And furthermore,
text: 他亲自挑选的参谋长联席会议主席一路上多次告诉 Trump，我们并不具备实现他所追求目标的能力，这里的“他”指的是 Donald Trump。而且，

speaker: Guest
text_en: Cain has told them that if we got into a war with Iran, we would run down our inventory of boutique weapons to a very dangerous point. And of course, that's where we are now. And that's one of the principal reasons. Reasons we don't want.
text: Cain 也告诉他们，如果我们和 Iran 开战，我们那些高端精确武器的库存会被消耗到非常危险的水平。当然，这也正是我们现在的处境。这也是最主要的原因之一。是我们不想

speaker: Guest
text_en: restart the war. We don't have the weaponry to wage it.
text: 重启这场战争。我们没有足够的武器装备来打这场仗。

speaker: Host
text_en: How dangerous is that point as we speak, Professor Mare Shimer? Do we know?
text: Professor Mare Shimer，就目前来说，这个危险水平到底有多严重？我们知道吗？

speaker: Guest
text_en: No, we don't know the details, but we do know that lots and lots of tomahawk missiles have been used, lots and lots of patriot missiles, thad missiles have been used,
text: 不知道，我们不了解具体细节，但我们知道，已经用了大量大量的 Tomahawk 导弹，大量大量的 Patriot 导弹，THAAD 导弹也用了不少，

speaker: Guest
text_en: all sorts of smart smart bombs have been used, and this has serious consequences for
text: 各种各样的智能炸弹都已经用了，这会对

speaker: Guest
text_en: containing China and East Asia as you've heard me say on the show before we were supposed to pivot to Asia to contain China but what we're doing is we're pivoting away from China we're bringing all sorts of military forces out of East Asia into the Gulf region because we need all the weapons we can get to fight against Iran and the problem that you face is when you expand these weapons you just can't replace them in a month's time
text: 遏制 China 以及 East Asia 的局势造成严重后果。你之前也听我在节目里说过，我们本来应该把战略重心转向亚洲来遏制 China，但我们现在做的却是把重心从 China 身上移开。我们正把各种军事力量从 East Asia 调往海湾地区，因为为了对付 Iran，我们需要尽可能多的武器。而你面临的问题是，这些武器一旦消耗掉，你根本不可能在一个月内补回来。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Once time or a year's time, it takes years to produce all the weapons that we've already used up and we don't want to be in a position where we use up more of them. But the point I'm making to you about General Kane is,
text: 别说一个月了，一年都不够。要把我们已经消耗掉的这些武器重新生产出来，需要很多年。我们不想让自己落到消耗更多武器的处境里。但我想跟你强调的关于 General Kane 的一点是，

speaker: Guest
text_en: unsurprisingly, he recognized this a long time ago and he's advised the president accordingly. But Trump didn't listen to him because Trump thought he could win a quick and decisive victory. But once he doesn't win a quick and decisive victory.
text: 不出所料，他很早以前就意识到了这一点，也据此向总统提出了建议。但 Trump 没听他的，因为 Trump 以为自己能迅速而决定性地取胜。可一旦他没能迅速而决定性地取胜，

speaker: Guest
text_en: victory he's in the deep soup and that's where we are today and of course this is why the military is now telling trump don't start this war let me put it differently don't start this bombing campaign up again because it's not going to work and of course if you don't do the bombing campaign and you have no ground forces option which we don't have you're reduced to relying on a naval blockade and then the question is do you think that this naval blockade is going to bring the iranians
text: 那他就陷入大麻烦了，而这正是我们今天的处境。当然，这也是为什么军方现在在告诉 Trump：别打这场仗。换句话说，别再重启这轮轰炸行动了，因为根本不会奏效。当然，如果你不搞轰炸行动，又没有地面部队这个选项——而我们确实没有——那你就只能依赖海上封锁。接下来问题就是，你觉得这种海上封锁能让伊朗人

speaker: Guest
text_en: to their knees all I can say is good luck on that one that's not going to happen either you can inflict huge amounts of punishment on Iranians and they will not throw their hands up they are facing an existential threat and when you're facing an existential threat you fight to the last person
text: 屈服吗？我只能说，祝你好运吧。这也不会发生。你可以给伊朗人造成巨大的打击，但他们不会举手投降。他们面对的是生存威胁，而当你面对生存威胁时，你会战斗到最后一个人。

speaker: Host
text_en: What will happen if the United States enters into some sort of a long-term peaceful agreement with Iran that excludes Israel?
text: 如果美国和伊朗达成某种长期和平协议，而且把 Israel 排除在外，会发生什么？

speaker: Guest
text_en: You know, I've thought about this, and I think the connective issue that makes it hard to separate those two dimensions of the problem is Iranian support for Hezbollah and Hamas.
text: 你知道，我想过这个问题。我觉得，让这个问题的这两个层面很难分开的关键连接点，是伊朗对 Hezbollah 和 Hamas 的支持。

speaker: Guest
text_en: The Israelis are going to continue to pound Hezbollah, just to pick on that example, and we see that happening right now. There's supposed to be a ceasefire, but in fact, there's no ceasefire. Hezbollah. on the idea for firing away at each other,
text: Israel 还会继续打击 Hezbollah，就拿这个例子来说，我们现在就看到了这种情况。按说应该停火了，但事实上根本没有停火。Hezbollah 和 Israel 还在继续互相开火，

speaker: Guest
text_en: and that's going to continue for as far as the eye can see. But the Iranians support Hezbollah, they support Hamas, and they support the Houthis. And the Iranians, under no circumstances, are going to give up the support for those three groups.
text: 而且在可预见的未来，这种情况都会持续下去。但伊朗支持 Hezbollah，支持 Hamas，也支持 Houthis。而且伊朗在任何情况下都不会放弃对这三个组织的支持。

speaker: Guest
text_en: That is going to make it very hard for the United States to reach a meaningful peace agreement with Iran because Israel is inextricable. strictly linked with Iran vis-a-vis those three groups. And the United States is, of course, linked with Israel.
text: 这会让美国很难和伊朗达成一项有实质意义的和平协议，因为在这三个组织的问题上，Israel 和伊朗是不可分割、紧密相连的。而美国当然又是和 Israel 绑定在一起的。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So I think all of this tells you you're not going to get a meaningful peace agreement here.
text: 所以我觉得，这一切都说明，你不可能在这里达成什么有实质意义的和平协议。

speaker: Guest
text_en: You may get, you know, some Rube Goldberg type agreement that, you know, puts out the fire for a while, but there's no real hope of a meaningful peace agreement. agreement. And this is in large part because of Israel.
text: 你也许会得到某种很拼凑、很复杂的那种协议，暂时把火压一压，但根本看不到达成真正有意义的和平协议的希望。这在很大程度上是因为 Israel。

speaker: Guest
text_en: As I've argued for a long time, actually with Steve Walt, you have to understand that Israel is a giant albatross around their neck. I think more and more Americans are coming to realize that.
text: 正如我长期以来一直在说的，实际上我和 Steve Walt 也这么论证过，你必须明白，Israel 对美国来说就是个沉重的大包袱。我觉得越来越多美国人开始意识到这一点了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But we are in deep trouble in the Middle East because of Israel, and we have no way of fixing the problem. The lobby is so powerful here in the United States when it comes to influencing policymakers.
text: 但我们在中东陷入了很深的麻烦，而这一切都是因为 Israel，而且我们根本没有办法解决这个问题。游说集团在美国影响政策制定者这件事上，力量实在太强了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: makers that it's just hard to make any progress in solving this problem and by the way the principal reason we're not moving towards some sort of ceasefire now some sort of even half-baked agreement is in large part because of Israel in the lobby they just will not let Trump cut any kind of meaningful deal with the Iranians they want the war to go on
text: 政策制定者这边也是一样，所以要想在解决这个问题上取得任何进展都非常困难。顺便说一句，我们现在之所以没有朝着某种停火、甚至某种哪怕不太成熟的协议迈进，主要也是因为 Israel 和游说集团。他们就是不肯让 Trump 和 Iranians 达成任何有实质意义的协议，他们希望战争继续打下去。

speaker: Host
text_en: So where will we be in your view? when six months or eight months.
text: 那按你的看法，六个月或八个月之后，局面会是什么样？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, we may have, you know, some sort of Rube Goldberg agreement, you know, that allows commerce to flow through the strait. There may be no blockade. The Iranians will be controlling the strait. I find it hard to believe that we'll have a nuclear.
text: 嗯，我们也许会有某种很拼凑的协议，让商业运输能够通过这条海峡。可能不会有封锁。Iranians 会控制这条海峡。我很难相信我们会达成一项核协议。

speaker: Guest
text_en: agreement, I hope were wrong. But if we do have a nuclear agreement, it's going to look something like the JCPOA, which
text: 核协议，我希望我是错的。但如果真有核协议，它看起来会有点像 JCPOA，也就是

speaker: Host
text_en: Right.
text: 对。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Iran is going to maintain the right and the ability to enrich uranium. And Israel is never going to accept that. The lobby is never going to accept that.
text: Iran 会坚持保有浓缩铀的权利和能力。而 Israel 永远不会接受这一点。那个游说集团也永远不会接受。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And the neoconservatives more generally in this country who are very powerful in policy circles are never going to accept.
text: 而且更广泛地说，这个国家里那些在政策圈影响力很大的新保守主义者，也永远不会接受。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So, you know, we're just going to go on and on arguing with the Iranians about their nuclear capability, threatening them and so forth and so on. And by the way, if you don't get a meaningful agreement on the nuclear issue,
text: 所以，你知道，我们只会没完没了地就 Iran 的核能力跟他们争论，威胁他们，诸如此类。顺便说一句，如果你们在核问题上达不成一项有实质意义的协议，

speaker: Guest
text_en: then we're going to run into the possibility that the Israelis may use nuclear weapons against Iran to deal with that problem. So the future here is.
text: 那我们就会面临一种可能性：Israel 可能会动用核武器来对付 Iran，解决这个问题。所以这里的未来是——

speaker: Guest
text_en: is bleak. And by the way, the future is bleak in Europe as well. The future in East Asia is bleak as well.
text: 很黯淡。顺便说一句，Europe 的未来也很黯淡。East Asia 的未来也一样黯淡。

speaker: Guest
text_en: We have made a complete hash of things. Of course, it's not only the result of the Trump administration's policies, although this Iran war has contributed greatly to that, but the whole disaster in Ukraine just goes on and on and on.
text: 我们把事情彻底搞砸了。当然，这不只是 Trump 政府政策造成的结果，尽管这场 Iran 战争对此推波助澜很大，但 Ukraine 的整场灾难也一直没完没了地持续着。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And that is going to have serious serious negative strategic consequences for as far as the eye can see that, as I pointed out before, are inextricably bound up with what's going on in the Gulf.
text: 而这将在可预见的未来带来极其严重的负面战略后果。正如我之前指出的那样，这些后果和 Gulf 正在发生的事情是密不可分地纠缠在一起的。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And then this, you know, leaves aside the whole question of East Asia, which is in many ways the most dangerous area of all because that's where the United States faces a peer competitor.
text: 然后，这还先不谈整个东亚问题。东亚在很多方面其实是最危险的地区，因为美国在那里面对的是一个同级别的竞争对手。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I shudder to think what would happen if we had a crisis in East Asia. I hope Trump can work with Xi Jinping to make sure that that that doesn't happen. But you want to remember,
text: 一想到如果东亚爆发危机会怎么样，我就不寒而栗。我希望 Trump 能和 Xi Jinping 合作，确保那种情况不会发生。不过你得记住，

speaker: Guest
text_en: we have two major wars taking place, one in the Gulf and the other in Europe, and we don't have a war in East Asia. But if we did, we would be in the really deep kimchi. And thankfully, we don't have that problem. And hopefully,
text: 现在有两场重大战争正在进行，一场在海湾地区，另一场在欧洲，而东亚还没有战争。但如果真有的话，我们可就真的陷入大麻烦了。谢天谢地，我们现在还没有这个问题。也希望，

speaker: Guest
text_en: President Trump can work something out with Xi so that that doesn't happen.
text: President Trump 能和 Xi 商量出个办法，避免那种情况发生。

speaker: Host
text_en: What can President Trump work out with President Putin? I mean, and does Putin have his own issues? Do you sense impatience on the part of Russian elites, Kremlin officials and average Russians with a four year war now?
text: President Trump 能和 President Putin 谈出什么结果？我的意思是，Putin 自己是不是也有他的难题？你有没有感觉到，俄罗斯精英、Kremlin 官员以及普通俄罗斯人，对这场已经打了四年的战争都开始不耐烦了？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Yeah, there's no question about that.
text: 对，这一点毫无疑问。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I've been thinking about the possibility that Russians, the Russians themselves might use nuclear weapons at some point. It's quite clear that the Europeans are doing everything they can to help the Ukrainians and the Ukrainians are getting quite effective at hitting targets inside of Russia. And the end result is Russian foreign policy elites and even the...
text: 我一直在想一种可能性，就是俄罗斯人自己某个时候可能会动用核武器。很明显，欧洲人正在竭尽所能帮助乌克兰人，而乌克兰人现在也越来越擅长打击俄罗斯境内的目标。最终的结果就是，俄罗斯的外交政策精英，甚至连……

speaker: Guest
text_en: Even the public are getting antsy about settling this war. They're putting great pressure on President Putin to put an end to it, and it's not easy to do that. I mean, even as the Russians capture more and more territory,
text: 甚至连普通民众，都开始急着想把这场战争了结掉。他们正在给 President Putin 施加巨大压力，要他结束这场战争，但这并不容易。我的意思是，即便俄罗斯人占领了越来越多的领土，

speaker: Guest
text_en: the fact is that the Ukrainians with all these drones and all the help they're getting from Europe and even the United States are able to attack targets in Russia. And the question is, what will the Russians do in response?
text: 事实是，乌克兰人靠着这些无人机，以及他们从欧洲、甚至美国得到的各种援助，已经能够打击俄罗斯境内的目标了。问题在于，俄罗斯人会如何回应？

speaker: Guest
text_en: And there's this very prominent strategist who I actually know reasonably well, Sergei Karaganov, who has been making the argument for a long time. long time that the Russians have to put an end to this.
text: 还有一位非常知名的战略家，我跟他其实还算熟，Sergei Karaganov，长期以来一直在主张，俄罗斯必须结束这种局面。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And the only way you can do that is to use nuclear weapons, not start a general thermonuclear war,
text: 而唯一能做到这一点的办法，就是动用核武器，不是发动一场全面的热核战争，

speaker: Guest
text_en: but use nuclear weapons against the West in a demonstrative way to signal to the West that enough is enough. And if they continue to support Ukraine and allow Ukraine to destroy Russian infrastructure, then what the Russians will do is they'll go up.
text: 而是以一种示范性的方式对西方使用核武器，向西方发出信号：够了。如果他们继续支持乌克兰，并允许乌克兰摧毁俄罗斯的基础设施，那么俄罗斯接下来就会升级。

speaker: Guest
text_en: blow up the nuclear escalation ladder. And Kara Ganov argues that once you use a few nuclear weapons against the West, they will get the message loudly and clearly, and they will cease and desist from supporting Ukraine.
text: 把核升级的阶梯往上推。Karaganov 认为，一旦对西方使用几枚核武器，他们就会清清楚楚地明白这个信号，也就会停止支持乌克兰。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But the key point here is you don't want to put the Russians in a desperate situation.
text: 但这里的关键点是，你不能把俄罗斯逼到绝境。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And of course, that's exactly what Europe is trying to do. As I was saying before, Sergey Lavrov gave a very important very important speech on April 24th, and it would be well worth people's while to go back and look at that speech.
text: 当然，欧洲现在恰恰就是在这么做。就像我之前说的，Sergey Lavrov 在 4 月 24 日发表了一场非常重要、非常重要的讲话，我觉得大家很值得回头去看看那场讲话。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But Lavrov makes it very clear that he believes, and of course he's reflecting a Russian perspective on this, that the West has declared war against Russia. He believes that Russia is at war with Russia. the West.
text: 但 Lavrov 说得非常明确，他认为——当然，这反映的是俄罗斯的视角——西方已经对俄罗斯宣战了。他认为，俄罗斯正在与西方交战。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And he believes that Russia's just not fighting Ukraine inside of Ukraine. Russia is also fighting against Europe. And he says very clearly that he believes that the Europeans are out to To achieve a strategic victory against Russia.
text: 而且他认为，Russia 不只是在 Ukraine 境内和 Ukraine 作战。Russia 其实也是在和 Europe 对抗。他说得非常明确：他相信 Europeans 的目标，是要在战略上战胜 Russia。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And what he basically is saying is that the Europeans, and of course this means the West in general, and he uses the term the West, wants to knock the Russians out of the ranks of the great powers.
text: 而他这番话的基本意思是，Europeans——当然，这其实也指整个 West，他自己用的也是 the West 这个说法——是想把 Russians 从大国行列里打出去。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And when you are pursuing a goal like that and you're inflicting significant damage on Russian infrastructure, you're going to give the Russians. significant incentives to get tough and maybe even use nuclear weapons.
text: 而当你在追求这样的目标，同时又对 Russian 的基础设施造成重大破坏时，你就是在给 Russians 很强的动机，逼他们采取强硬手段，甚至可能动用核武器。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I didn't think we'd be at this point. I'm actually quite surprised that I'm saying what I'm saying. But I think one does not want to underestimate what's going on in Ukraine and how dangerous that situation is. And again,
text: 我原本没想到局势会走到这一步。说实话，我自己也很惊讶，我现在竟然会说出这样的话。但我认为，绝不能低估 Ukraine 正在发生的事情，也不能低估那里的局势有多危险。还有，

speaker: Guest
text_en: getting back to the Middle East, I was talking about the possibility that Israel might use nuclear weapons. I'm not saying that's likely any more than I'm saying that it's likely the Russians will use nuclear weapons.
text: 回到 Middle East 的话题，我刚才提到过 Israel 可能会使用核武器。我并不是说这件事很可能发生，就像我也不是在说 Russians 很可能会使用核武器一样。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But you do not want to underestimate. The radical steps that states will take if they feel desperate. And you can hypothesize plausible situations where both the Israelis and the Russians feel desperate and think about using nuclear weapons.
text: 但你绝不能低估，一个国家在感到走投无路时会采取多么激进的行动。你完全可以设想一些合理的情境：无论是 Israelis 还是 Russians，都可能陷入绝境，并开始考虑动用核武器。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And by the way, you want to understand that if Israel were to use nuclear weapons against Iran, Iran does not have the capability to retaliate. And if Russia were to use nuclear weapons inside of Ukraine,
text: 顺便说一句，你要明白，如果 Israel 对 Iran 使用核武器，Iran 是没有能力进行报复的。而如果 Russia 在 Ukraine 境内使用核武器，

speaker: Guest
text_en: the Ukrainians can't retaliate. So this is a very dangerous situation. situation that we're in. And if you think about our discussion on this show and your discussion with our colleagues and other shows that you have during the week,
text: Ukrainians 也没有能力报复。所以我们现在面对的是一个非常危险的局面。再想想我们今天这期节目里的讨论，以及你这周在其他节目里和我们同事的讨论，

speaker: Guest
text_en: I think everybody is very pessimistic about where all these conflicts are headed. Nobody, you know, among us can tell a happy story about how any of these conflicts end. And this, I think, should make all of us extremely worried.
text: 我觉得，大家对这些冲突接下来会走向哪里都非常悲观。我们这些人里，说实话，没人能讲出一个圆满的结局，说明这些冲突会怎么收场。而我认为，这应该让我们所有人都极其担忧。

speaker: Host
text_en: And worried we are. Professor Mearsheimer, thank you very much. Thanks for that extraordinary analysis on both counts, on both Iran and Ukraine. We'll look forward to seeing you next week, my dear friend.
text: 而我们也确实很担忧。Mearsheimer 教授，非常感谢你。感谢你就这两件事——Iran 和 Ukraine——所做的精彩分析。亲爱的朋友，我们期待下周再见到你。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I look forward to it as well, Judge.
text: 我也很期待，Judge。

speaker: Host
text_en: Thank you. Coming up later today at one o'clock on all of this, the golden boy, Max Blumenthal at two o'clock.
text: 谢谢。今天晚些时候，关于这一切的更多内容，一点钟播出；两点钟是金童 Max Blumenthal。

speaker: Host
text_en: Maco, are American troops willing to kill innocents knowingly? And at three o'clock, Colonel Karen Kwiatkowski writes a fascinating piece up at Judge Knapp. Are we on the abyss? Are we on a cliff of the insane? Judge Napolitano for judging freedom.
text: Maco，美国军人会明知对方是无辜者还下手杀人吗？还有，三点钟，Colonel Karen Kwiatkowski 在 Judge Knapp 上发表了一篇很精彩的文章。我们是不是已经站在深渊边上了？是不是已经站在疯狂的悬崖边了？我是 Judge Napolitano，这里是 Judging Freedom。
